name: Lagunas y Río Torote – circular desde Daganzo de Arriba
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Daganzo De Arriba traverse a landscape characterized by river plains, forest parks, and gentle rolling terrain. The region features areas like the Lagunas y Río Torote, offering routes along water bodies and through natural areas. Forested sections, such as those found near Parque Forestal Maria Marzol, provide shaded paths. The local topography includes modest elevation changes, suitable for various mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Daganzo De Arriba The most popular mountain bike trail is Lagunas y Río Torote – circular desde Daganzo de Arriba, a 30.6 miles (49.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route explores the area's lagoons and river. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Fountain of the Four Paths – View of Cobeña loop from Daganzo de Arriba, a moderate 24.8 miles (39.9 km) path. This route offers views towards Cobeña and passes through varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Serrucho de Daganzo – Alalpardo Viewpoint loop from Daganzo de Arriba, a 26.1 miles (42.0 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around Daganzo De Arriba is defined by river valleys, forest parks, and open plains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging ability levels. The routes in Daganzo De Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Daganzo De Arriba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Daganzo De Arriba?
A: There are over 35 mountain bike trails available around Daganzo De Arriba, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Daganzo De Arriba?
A: The terrain around Daganzo De Arriba is characterized by river plains, forest parks, and gentle rolling hills. You'll find routes along water bodies like the Lagunas y Río Torote, and shaded paths through forested sections such as those near Parque Forestal Maria Marzol. The elevation changes are generally modest.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Daganzo De Arriba?
A: Yes, Daganzo De Arriba offers several easy mountain bike trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14895449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Forestal Maria Marzol loop from Daganzo de Arriba&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 23.7 km route with minimal elevation gain, perfect for those new to mountain biking.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: While most routes are moderate, experienced riders can find longer, more demanding moderate trails that offer a good workout. The region's topography provides varied terrain to keep things interesting, with some routes featuring more significant elevation changes over longer distances.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Daganzo De Arriba?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Daganzo De Arriba are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963971508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas y Río Torote – circular desde Daganzo de Arriba&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 49.2 km loop exploring the area's natural lagoons and river.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the trails?
A: Along the trails, you can explore natural areas like the Lagunas y Río Torote. Nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2663351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Europa Monuments in Torrejón de Ardoz&lt;/a&gt;, the historical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2393087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3221681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge Over the Henares River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Many outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural parks or areas with wildlife, and to check specific regulations for each trail or park you plan to visit. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Daganzo De Arriba?
A: Yes, the region offers trails suitable for families, particularly the easier routes with less elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14895449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Forestal Maria Marzol loop from Daganzo de Arriba&lt;/a&gt; is a good option for families looking for a less strenuous ride through pleasant forested areas.

Q: Where can I park when visiting the mountain bike trails in Daganzo De Arriba?
A: Daganzo De Arriba has various parking options within the town. For specific trailheads, you may find designated parking areas or roadside parking. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for the most convenient parking suggestions.

Q: Is Daganzo De Arriba accessible by public transport for mountain bikers?
A: Daganzo De Arriba is connected by regional bus services from Madrid. While buses can get you to the town, transporting a mountain bike on public transport might have restrictions. It's recommended to check with the local transport provider for their specific policies regarding bicycles.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the mountain bike trails?
A: Daganzo De Arriba town center offers various cafes, bars, and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. Some routes might pass through or near other villages with amenities, but it's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer rides.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Daganzo De Arriba?
A: The mountain bike trails in Daganzo De Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic river and lagoon areas, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Daganzo De Arriba?
A: Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Daganzo De Arriba, with mild temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or cold. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winter can be cooler but often still suitable for riding.
tips: Recreational area with the Virgen del Espino Hermitage, nearby we have the Totrote River, a shaded area and tables to eat
Agricultural road with a good surface to ride and with a beautiful landscape. It allows to connect from Alcalá de Henares to the town of Sarracines through the sown fields.
Nice lagoon on the bed of the Torote river a few km from Sarracines. It can be reached from both sides of the river by path or track, whichever you prefer.
Area with great views of the Henares valley. Up and down route to test your legs on the bike.

It is a practically completely abandoned town except for some buildings that are still used for storage. It is picturesque in its surroundings and it is curious to give it a tour imagining how it was lived there at some point.

Perfect viewpoint over Madrid and the Henares corridor in the middle of the cereal steppe, the path ends here since the farm through which it continues has currently been blocked, it is worth going up and retracing our steps just for the views.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint at the Geodetic Vertex of the Cereal Steppes
